breakout_products.title: Someone just open-sourced a god-tier OSINT dashboard. It's called Shadowbroker. It pulls every op...
breakout_products.content: Someone just open-sourced a god-tier OSINT dashboard. It's called Shadowbroker. It pulls every open military signal on Earth into one map, carrier strike groups, spy satellites, GPS jamming zones, 25,000+ ships, 2,000+ live CCTV feeds. Right-click anywhere on Earth. Get a full intelligence dossier. 100% Open Source.
breakout_products.selection_reason: Powerful open-source OSINT dashboard with real-time global intelligence data integration.
breakout_products.summary: Shadowbroker is a newly released open-source intelligence dashboard that aggregates global military signals, satellite data, and live feeds into a single interactive map.
breakout_products.display_title: Shadowbroker Releases Open-Source OSINT Dashboard for Mapping Global Military Signals and Live Feeds
key_points: The platform aggregates data on carrier strike groups, spy satellites, and GPS jamming zones globally.
Users can right-click any location on the map to instantly generate a comprehensive intelligence dossier.
The system tracks over 25,000 ships and integrates more than 2,000 live CCTV feeds.

breakout_products.title: Flowly
breakout_products.content: Your personal AI assistant, native to your desktop. Flowly is a native AI assistant that takes action across your apps and browser tabs. Summon it as a full chat, from the menubar, or through a notch overlay — one global hotkey away on macOS, Windows, and Linux. Available for macOS, Windows, Linux. Free during launch. Product categories: AI Voice Agents, AI Workflow Automation, OpenClaw.

Flowly is a native AI assistant that takes action across your apps and browser tabs.

breakout_products.title: 𝚗𝚙𝚡 𝚍𝚎𝚎𝚙𝚜𝚎𝚌 We're introducing an open-source agent orchestrator for deep security reviews. We bui...
breakout_products.content: 𝚗𝚙𝚡 𝚍𝚎𝚎𝚙𝚜𝚎𝚌 We're introducing an open-source agent orchestrator for deep security reviews. We built it for internal use, and after running it against some major OSS projects, we gained conviction to share it with the world. Coding agents can now find critical vulnerabilities in minutes that would take teams of people months (if they can spot them at all). Since 𝚍𝚎𝚎𝚙𝚜𝚎𝚌 is optimized to work with Vercel Sandbox, you can effectively harness the power of thousands of agents scrutinizing your codebase in parallel. I encourage you to try this on your repositories. BTW: If you run an OSS project and want us to sponsor a run, my DMs are open.
breakout_products.selection_reason: New open-source agent orchestrator for automated security reviews
breakout_products.summary: Originally developed for internal use, this open-source agent orchestrator leverages parallel coding agents to rapidly identify critical security vulnerabilities in codebases.
breakout_products.display_title: New Open-Source Agent Orchestrator Automates Deep Security Reviews Across Codebases in Minutes
key_points: The tool utilizes coding agents to discover critical vulnerabilities in minutes rather than the months typically required by human teams.
It is optimized for Vercel Sandbox, enabling developers to run thousands of scrutinizing agents simultaneously.
The creators are currently offering sponsored security review runs for open-source projects upon request.

breakout_products.title: Manex
breakout_products.content: Preserve useful answers, corrections, and context as memory. Manex is a private AI memory for documents and team knowledge. Upload files, ask grounded questions, and preserve useful answers, corrections, and context as memory. It runs locally where supported, keeps data private by default, and lets teams create a shared brain without per-seat pricing. Product categories: Knowledge base software.

llm.title: 🎙️ Voice AI only feels natural when conversation keeps pace with speech. Here’s how we rebuilt ou...
llm.content: 🎙️ Voice AI only feels natural when conversation keeps pace with speech. Here’s how we rebuilt our WebRTC stack with a thin relay and stateful transceiver to keep real-time media fast for ChatGPT voice, the Realtime API, and more. https://t.co/JEvs2PmsmC
llm.selection_reason: Technical deep dive into OpenAI's low-latency WebRTC stack for real-time voice.

llm.display_title: OpenAI Details WebRTC Stack Rebuild for Low-Latency Real-Time Voice AI Interactions
key_points: The rebuilt stack utilizes a thin relay and a stateful transceiver to accelerate media processing.
These infrastructure changes directly power the low-latency performance of ChatGPT voice features.
The optimizations also support developers building with the Realtime API.

llm.title: Researchers at EPFL proved your AI is lying to you. Not sometimes. Most of the time. They built o...
llm.content: Researchers at EPFL proved your AI is lying to you. Not sometimes. Most of the time. They built one of the hardest hallucination tests ever made with Max Planck Institute. 950 questions. Four domains where being wrong actually hurts. Legal. Medical. Research. Coding. Then they ran every top model on it. The results. GPT-5. Wrong 71.8% of the time. Claude Opus 4.5. Wrong 60% of the time. Gemini 3 Pro. Wrong 61.9% of the time. DeepSeek Reasoner. Wrong 76.8% of the time. These are the smartest AI models on Earth. The ones you trust with your career. Your health. Your money. You think turning on web search fixes it. It doesn't. Claude Opus 4.5 with web search. Still wrong 30.2% of the time. GPT-5.2 thinking with web search. Still wrong 38.2% of the time. The internet attached. Still lying to you in 1 out of every 3 answers. Now the part that should scare you. Medical questions. The one place being wrong can kill you. GPT-5 hallucinated 92.8% of the time on medical guidelines. Claude Haiku 4.5 hallucinated 95.7% of the time. Gemini 3 Flash hallucinated 89% of the time. Nine out of ten medical answers from popular AI models. Wrong. It gets worse. The longer you talk to it, the more it lies. Early mistakes cascade. The model starts citing its own earlier hallucinations as facts. Your third message is more wrong than your first. The paper, in its own words: "hallucinations remain substantial even with web search." This is what hundreds of millions of people are doing right now. Asking software that lies in the majority of its answers. About their health. About their job. About their legal case. About their code. Most are not checking. Most never will. But please. Keep using ChatGPT for medical advice. The doctors need a break. https://t.co/dHBP5CDpTM
llm.selection_reason: Critical research from EPFL/Max Planck on high hallucination rates in top-tier LLMs.
llm.summary: A new study by EPFL and the Max Planck Institute demonstrates that leading AI models, including GPT-5 and Claude Opus 4.5, frequently hallucinate when answering questions in high-stakes fields like medicine and law.
llm.display_title: EPFL Study Reveals High Hallucination Rates in Top AI Models Across Critical Domains
key_points: Researchers tested models on 950 questions across legal, medical, research, and coding domains.
GPT-5 hallucinated 71.8% of the time overall and 92.8% on medical guidelines.
Adding web search reduced errors but models still hallucinated in roughly one-third of their answers.

llm.title: Stochastic KV Routing: Enabling Adaptive Depth-Wise Cache Sharing
llm.content: Serving transformer language models with high throughput requires caching Key-Values (KVs) to avoid redundant computation during autoregressive generation. The memory footprint of KV caching is significant and heavily impacts serving costs. This work proposes to lessen these m...
llm.selection_reason: Apple research on optimizing KV cache memory for more efficient transformer serving.
llm.summary: Apple researchers have developed a new method called stochastic KV routing to decrease the significant memory footprint associated with caching key-values during autoregressive generation.
llm.display_title: Apple introduces stochastic KV routing to reduce memory footprint and serving costs for transformer language models
key_points: High-throughput transformer serving requires caching key-values to prevent redundant computation.
The large memory footprint of KV caching significantly increases operational serving costs.
The proposed adaptive depth-wise cache sharing aims to mitigate these memory demands.
